text:
Concepts, principles and values that can be derived from the verses 23-24 of Surah Al-Israa’ provide a basic foundation for Islamic pedagogy which is taught to the families for establishing the faith of tawhid and for developing their love and obedience towards their parents. This study examines the concept and the importance of Islamic education and the moral values which are derived from these two verses in relation to the students’ obedient behaviour towards their parents and its effects on the students’ religious character building. According to library research methodology with content analysis technique and by reviewing the related literature from primary and secondary sources; the Tafsir of the Quran and the Hadiths, the necessary information is derived and interpreted. Based on this information, the basic principles of the Islamic education which are considered in these verses, are determined as being three: - the education of having the eternal and absolute loving towards one’s parents - the education of addressing the parents with the utmost respect - the education of the worship by making a mediator (shafi) between the parent and Allah by means of du’a and in favour of one’s parents. By adopting the concepts of “birrul walidain” - which are referred to the moral values of the students’ behaviours towards their parents derived from the mentioned verses of the Surah Al-Israa’- it is observed that the students’ characters were strongly developed due to their adopting these values and as a result they exhibited attributes of patience, humbleness, avoidance of harm and committing to pray for their parents throughout their lifetime and even after their death.

**Keywords:** Islamic Education, Quran, Religious Character
